title: Sesame Ginger Noodles
subtitle: with Stir-Fried Greens & Toasted Peanuts

steps.text: Add 2 quarts water and 1 tbsp salt to a medium saucepan and bring to a boil. Chop gai lan into 1 inch pieces. Peel and mince 2 tsp ginger. Trim and thinly slice the mini sweet peppers.
Add the minced ginger, tamari, peanut butter, rice vinegar, just half the sesame oil, just half the chili garlic sauce, and ¼ hot water to a medium bowl and whisk the sesame ginger sauce.

Add the udon to the boiling water and cook until al dente, 6 to 8 minutes. Drain noodles and run under cold water to stop the cooking process. Heat the remaining sesame oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add peanuts and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook until nuts are toasted and fragrant, 1 to 3 minutes. Use a slotted spoon to transfer the toasted peanuts to a small bowl, leaving the oil in the skillet.

Return the skillet with sesame oil to high heat. Thinly slice the Swiss chard. Add chopped gai lan, sliced Swiss chard, and a pinch of salt and pepper to the skillet. Cook until bright green and tender, 2 to 3 minutes.
Add the cooked udon and sesame ginger sauce to the skillet with the greens. Cook, tossing frequently, until evenly coated and warmed through, 1 to 3 minutes.
Divide the sesame ginger noodles and stir-fried greens between bowls. Top with sliced mini sweet peppers and toasted peanuts. Enjoy!

tips: TIP: Save any remaining ginger for your own use.
TIPS: The sauce will not be completely smooth. Keep any remaining chili garlic sauce for your own use.
* These ingredients will be split between steps.
